articles.title: Kazakh Oil Flows to Black Sea Forced to Stop as Tankers Hit
articles.summary: Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast as a spate of attacks on tankers jeopardizes the landlocked Central Asian country’s exports.

articles.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion Graffiti Towers Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
articles.summary: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more t

articles.title: Chinese Oil Tanker U-Turns in Red Sea After Houthi Attack Threat
articles.summary: A very large crude carrier has made a U-turn in the southern Red Sea after warnings from Houthi rebels in Yemen that they would target ships heading to or from Saudi ports.

articles.title: Economic Momentum Flips From Blessing to Curse for S&P 500 Bulls
articles.summary: Strong economic momentum is a double-edged sword for US equity traders. Solid growth is an earnings tailwind. But let it run too hot, and it turns from blessing to curse.

articles.title: Shale Trades Go-Go Days of Growth for Output Creep
articles.summary: US drillers are letting production rise again — just not the way they used to.

articles.title: Wall Street Talks Up Carry Trade as Returns Soar Most in Decades
articles.summary: One of the most enduring foreign-exchange bets is off to its best run in decades as surprisingly muted volatility across asset classes has investors piling into carry trades.

articles.title: Stocks Rise as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Dip buyers extended a rebound in chipmakers, lifting global stock markets. Brent topped $90 a barrel as the US and Iran exchanged strikes for a 10th straight day.

articles.title: Victoria Debt Deal Faces Opposition From HSBC Bank
articles.summary: UK carpet maker Victoria Plc.’s bid to restructure its balance sheet is facing resistance from HSBC Holdings Plc, a holder of the company’s bonds.

articles.title: The Great British Takeover Tops $60 Billion After Mitie Deal
articles.summary: The UK stock market is losing the equivalent of more than $2 billion a week to takeovers of London-listed companies, with Mitie Group Plc the latest name to be snapped up.

articles.title: Tether’s Three-Way Crypto Deal Is Called Off, Mallers Steps Down
articles.summary: A proposed merger of three crypto firms has been scrapped, with one of their leaders stepping down and another consolidating power across the Tether-backed franchise.
